zh.lifeSummary: 當Humphrey Hewster於1420年出生在英國英格蘭素可伯頓 煙雨 科茨時，他的父親John Brewster40歲，母親Elizabeth Joseph20歲。他於1445年與Joan de Burton在英國英格蘭薩默塞特伯頓結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1496年在他的家鄉享年76歲。

nameMeanings.name: Hewster
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German and Dutch: topographic name meaning ‘beech tree’ (see   Hester   1), or a habitational name from any of several places so named, particularly in North Rhine-Westphalia.

variants.name: Hiester
meanings: probably a habitational name from a place so named near Celle.

variants.name: Hester
meanings: North German: topographic name for someone who lived by a conspicuous beech tree, Middle Low German hēster, Middle High German heister ‘young tree (especially beech or oak)’. Compare Heister .
English: variant of Easter with prosthetic H-.

Irish (Mayo and Roscommon): possibly a shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hOistir ‘descendant of Oistir’, a personal name of unexplained etymology. However, without any early bearers in Gaelic sources, it may simply be the English name in Ireland.

nameMeanings.name: Humphrey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Norman name, Hunfrid, of Germanic origin, derived from hun ‘bear-cub, warrior’ + fred, frid ‘peace’. The Norman form absorbed the native Old English form, Hunfrith, which existed in England before the Conquest. The spelling with -ph- reflects classicizing influence. Since the 16th century it has enjoyed a modest popularity in England, having been borne most notably by the youngest son of King Henry IV , the Duke of Gloucester ( 1391–1447 ), known as ‘Duke Humphrey’. He was noted as a patron of literature, and founded what became the Bodleian Library at Oxford. In modern times, probably the most famous bearer has been the film star Humphrey Bogart ( 1899–1957 ).

variants.name: Humphry
meanings: From a Norman name, Hunfrid, of Germanic origin, derived from hun ‘bear-cub, warrior’ + fred, frid ‘peace’. The Norman form absorbed the native Old English form, Hunfrith, which existed in England before the Conquest. The spelling with -ph- reflects classicizing influence. Since the 16th century it has enjoyed a modest popularity in England, having been borne most notably by the youngest son of King Henry IV , the Duke of Gloucester ( 1391–1447 ), known as ‘Duke Humphrey’. en.lifeSummary: When Humphrey Hewster was born in 1420, in Burton with Coates, Sussex, England, United Kingdom, his father, John Brewster, was 40 and his mother, Elizabeth Joseph, was 20. He married Joan de Burton in 1445, in Burton, Somerset, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died in 1496, in his hometown, at the age of 76.

ko.lifeSummary: Humphrey Hewster(이)가 1420년 영국 잉글랜드 수세스 버턴 위스 코츠에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Brewster(은)는 40세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Joseph(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1445년 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 버턴에서 Joan de Burton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1496년 그의 고향에서 76세의 나이로 사망했다.
